description_long: This is a multi-story apartment building with a distinctive V-shaped footprint and a sloped, terracotta-colored roof. The exterior walls are finished in a light, off-white color, punctuated by regularly spaced rectangular windows on each floor. Each level features prominent, angular balconies with matching terracotta-colored undersides and railings, providing outdoor space for residents. The building's facade is symmetrical, with the main entrance located at the inner corner of the V, sheltered by the overhanging upper floors. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from concrete and masonry, with glass windows and metal balcony railings. The overall design is functional and modern, suitable for urban residential neighborhoods.
description: A V-shaped, multi-story apartment building with terracotta roof, light walls, and angular balconies on each floor. The facade is symmetrical with regular windows and a sheltered main entrance.
description_view_0: This view highlights the sloped terracotta roof and the angular balconies on the building's side, showing the depth of the balconies and the overhang of the roof.
description_view_1: This view shows the V-shaped layout most clearly, with the regular arrangement of windows and balconies along both wings, and the main entrance area at the inner corner.
description_view_2: This view focuses on the building's end, emphasizing the vertical stack of balconies and the solid, windowless side wall.
description_view_3: This view provides a clear look at the front facade, showing the symmetry of the windows and balconies, as well as the sheltered main entrance at the V's inner corner.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a regular, symmetrical grid pattern on each floor, with even spacing and alignment both vertically and horizontally.
